# IDataTableProps

Copyright (c) 2020, 2021, 2022 Visa, Inc.

This source code is licensed under the MIT license
https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/blob/master/LICENSE

## Properties

- `uniqueID: string`
  - ID used to identify chart (must be unique), helpful for validation messages. Defaults to UUID v4 standard.
- `data: object[]`
  - Chart data used to create table
- `secondaryData: object[]`
  - Chart data used to create a secondary table, if necessary
- `tableColumns: object[]`
  - Chart columns used to order columns in data table
- `secondaryTableColumns: object[]`
  - Chart columns used to order columns in secondary data table, if it exists
- `dataKeyNames: object`
  - Enables custom, user friendly names for data keys
- `isCompact: boolean`
  - When selected, applies compact height to rows of table
- `hideDataTable: boolean`
  - When selected, applies screen reader class to table button
- `padding: IBoxModelType`
  - Padding between plot area and axes lines
- `margin: IBoxModelType`
  - Margin between the subtitle and the chart area, or between the title and chart area if no subtitle is specified

*Source: `packages/data-table/src/components/data-table/data-table-props.ts`*

# ILineChartProps

Copyright (c) 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024 Visa, Inc.

This source code is licensed under the MIT license
https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/blob/master/LICENSE

## Properties

- `localization: ILocalizationType`
  - Internationalization properties of the chart
- `highestHeadingLevel: string | number`
  - Sets the heading level (which also sets sublevels) for the chart. "p", "span", and "div" are also valid.
- `mainTitle: string`
  - Main title of the chart
- `subTitle: string | ISubTitleType`
  - Subtitle of the chart. A string or an array of objects. Objects should contain text (one/multiple word(s)/number(s) as a single string) and color (HEX) keys. Optional key: index and mode (text | background).
- `height: number`
  - Height of the chart area in pixels
- `width: number`
  - Width of the chart area in pixels
- `margin: IBoxModelType`
  - Margin between the subtitle and the chart area, or between the title and chart area if no subtitle is specified
- `accessibility: IAccessibilityType`
  - Manages messages and settings for chart accessibility
- `animationConfig: IAnimationConfig`
  - Manages settings for chart animation, property 'disabled' defaults to false or undefined
- `padding: IBoxModelType`
  - Padding between plot area and axes lines
- `data: object[]`
  - Data used to create chart
- `uniqueID: string`
  - ID used to identify chart (must be unique), helpful for validation messages. Defaults to UUID v4 standard.
- `ordinalAccessor: string`
  - Key used to determine line's categorical property
- `valueAccessor: string`
  - Key used to determine line's numeric property
- `seriesAccessor: string`
  - Key used to determine series
- `xAxis: IAxisType`
  - Controls the visibility of the x-axis, the visibility of the grid, and the axis label and formatting
- `yAxis: IAxisType`
  - Controls the visibility of the y-axis, the visibility of the grid, and the axis label and formatting
- `showBaselineX: boolean`
  - When selected, shows the x baseline
- `wrapLabel: boolean`
  - When selected, wraps the ordinal tick labels
- `legend: ILegendType`
  - Sets visibility and label of chart legend
- `colorPalette: string`
  - Sets the color palette of the lines (overridden by colors)
- `hoverStyle: IHoverStyleType`
  - Changes the stroke width of line on hover
- `clickStyle: IClickStyleType`
  - Sets the styling of a bars when they are selected
- `referenceStyle: IReferenceStyleType`
  - Sets the styling of the reference line
- `annotations: object[]`
  - Adds annotations to the chart, see d3-annotation
- `colors: string[]`
  - Accepts array of color strings or values to customize line colors
- `strokeWidth: number`
  - Stroke width of series lines
- `showDots: boolean`
  - When selected, adds data points to line chart
- `dotRadius: number`
  - Sets the radius of data points, if present
- `lineCurve: string`
  - Sets the curve of the line, default is linear
- `secondaryLines: ISecondaryType`
  - Array of values used to classify series as secondary
- `hoverOpacity: number`
  - Sets the opacity of all other lines other than the one the mouse is over
- `cursor: string`
  - Changes pointer type during mouse over on lines
- `dataLabel: IDataLabelType`
  - Controls visibility, styling and placement of data labels
- `dataKeyNames: object`
  - Enables custom, user friendly names for data keys
- `seriesLabel: ISeriesLabelType`
  - Controls visibility, styling and placement of series labels
- `showTooltip: boolean`
  - When selected, allows tooltips to be displayed
- `tooltipLabel: ITooltipLabel`
  - Customize the tooltip content
- `maxValueOverride: number`
  - Overrides the calculated default max value
- `minValueOverride: number`
  - Overrides the calculated default min value
- `referenceLines: object[]`
  - Data that sets the location and labeling of the reference line(s)
- `interactionKeys: string[]`
  - Sets the column names of data to interact with
- `suppressEvents: boolean`
  - Suppresses and disables all event emitters. Setting to true can increase performance for non-interactive charts.
- `clickEvent: any`
  - Set a callback for click event on each data point.
- `hoverEvent: any`
  - Set a callback to execute when mouse enters the chart.
- `mouseOutEvent: any`
  - Set a callback to execute when mouse leaves the chart.
- `initialLoadEvent: any`
  - Set a callback to execute when chart initially loads.
- `initialLoadEndEvent: any`
  - Set a callback to execute when chart's initial load ends.
- `drawStartEvent: any`
  - Set a callback to execute when chart drawing starts.
- `drawEndEvent: any`
  - Set a callback to execute when chart drawing ends.
- `transitionEndEvent: any`
  - Set a callback to execute when chart geometry transitions end.
- `hoverHighlight: object`
  - * @controlName TextArea
- `clickHighlight: object[]`
  - * @controlName TextArea

*Source: `packages/line-chart/src/components/line-chart/line-chart-props.ts`*

# calculateLuminance

This function will calculate the perceived luminance of a valid HTML color.

Formula and specification from WCAG 2.0: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG20-TECHS/G18.html#G18-tests.

## Parameters

- `color: string`
  - Any valid HTML string may be passed for color: 'red', '#ffffff', 'rgb(12,200,15)', even 'hsl(0,60%,50%)'. Alpha values will be ignored (for accessibility purposes, web authors are strongly encouraged to avoid using opacity or alpha channels in styling).

## Returns

`number` - Returns a luminance value between 0 (black, no luminance) and 1 (white, full luminance).

*Source: `packages/utils/src/utils/colors.ts`*

# calculateRelativeLuminance

This function will calculate the contrast ratio between two luminance values.

Input luminance values may be supplied in any order. The output will always place the highest value as the numerator. Formula and specification from WCAG 2.0: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG20-TECHS/G18.html#G18-tests.

## Parameters

- `luminance1: number`
  - This value must be the calculated luminance of a color.
- `luminance2: number`
  - This value must be the calculated luminance of another color.

## Returns

`number` - Returns a contrast ratio between luminance values ranging from 1 (identical luminance) to 21 (complete opposites - black against white).

*Source: `packages/utils/src/utils/colors.ts`*

# autoTextColor

This function will find the most appropriate text color (foreground) given a background color.

This function is designed with VGAR compliance in mind, using the WCAG 2 contrast ratio formula to find the highest contrast text possible. Formula: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG20-TECHS/G18.html#G18-tests

## Parameters

- `backgroundColor: string`
  - Any valid HTML string may be passed for color: 'red', '#ffffff', 'rgb(12,200,15)', even 'hsl(0,60%,50%)'. Alpha values will be ignored (for accessibility purposes, web authors are strongly encouraged to avoid using opacity or alpha channels in styling).

## Returns

`string` - Returns a color hex value, either '#ffffff' (white) or '#222222' (lead).

## WorldMap Props

- **localization**: `ILocalizationType`
  - Internationalization properties of the chart
- **highestHeadingLevel**: `string | number`
  - Sets the heading level (which also sets sublevels) for the chart. "p", "span", and "div" are also valid.
- **mainTitle**: `string`
  - Main title of the chart
- **subTitle**: `string | ISubTitleType`
  - Subtitle of the chart. A string or an array of objects. Objects should contain text (one/multiple word(s)/number(s) as a single string) and color (HEX) keys. Optional key: index and mode (text | background).
- **height**: `number`
  - Height of the chart area in pixels
- **width**: `number`
  - Width of the chart area in pixels
- **margin**: `IBoxModelType`
  - Margin between the subtitle and the chart area, or between the title and chart area if no subtitle is specified
- **padding**: `IBoxModelType`
  - Padding between plot area and axes lines
- **data**: `object[]`
  - Data used to create chart
- **uniqueID**: `string`
  - ID used to identify chart (must be unique), helpful for validation messages. Defaults to UUID v4 standard.
- **annotations**: `object[]`
  - Adds annotations to the chart, see d3-annotation
- **markerAccessor**: `string`
  - Key used to determine marker's key property
- **markerNameAccessor**: `string`
  - Key used to determine marker's name property
- **joinAccessor**: `string`
  - Key used to determine country's key property (ISO 3-Digit Code)
- **joinNameAccessor**: `string`
  - Key used to determine country's name property
- **valueAccessor**: `string`
  - Key used to determine the country/marker's numeric property
- **latitudeAccessor**: `string`
  - Key used to determine marker's latitude property
- **longitudeAccessor**: `string`
  - Key used to determine marker's longitude property
- **sortOrder**: `string`
  - Can be used to sort the markers ascending/descending to ensure they are layered correctly
- **minValueOverride**: `number`
  - Overrides the calculated default min value for marker/color scales
- **maxValueOverride**: `number`
  - Overrides the calculated default max value for marker/color scales
- **groupAccessor**: `string`
  - Key used to determine country/marker color
- **mapProjection**: `string`
  - Sets the projection of the world-map to display * = Recommened for use.
- **mapScaleZoom**: `number`
  - Adjusts the zoom of the map (take caution when modifying this prop).
- **colorPalette**: `string`
  - Sets the color palette of the countries/markers. Overridden by colors.
- **quality**: `string`
  - Sets the quality of the map's vector shapes.
- **colors**: `string[]`
  - Accepts array of color strings or values to customize colors.
- **colorSteps**: `number`
  - Specifies the number of steps to use from colorPalette, overridden by colors.
- **markerStyle**: `IMarkerStyleType`
  - Object for setting various style attribute for map dots/markers
- **countryStyle**: `ICountryStyleType`
  - Object for setting various style attribute for countries
- **hoverStyle**: `IHoverStyleType`
  - Sets the color and opacity of marker/countries during mouse over
- **clickStyle**: `IClickStyleType`
  - Sets the styling of markers/countries when they are selected
- **hoverOpacity**: `number`
  - Sets opacity of element on hovering it
- **cursor**: `string`
  - Changes pointer type during mouse over on countries/markers
- **legend**: `ILegendType`
  - Sets visibility and label of chart legend
- **accessibility**: `IAccessibilityType`
  - Manages messages and settings for chart accessibility
- **animationConfig**: `IAnimationConfig`
  - Manages settings for chart animation, property 'disabled' defaults to false or undefined
- **dataLabel**: `IDataLabelType`
  - Controls visibility, styling and placement of data labels
- **dataKeyNames**: `object`
  - Enables custom, user friendly names for data keys
- **showTooltip**: `boolean`
  - When selected, allows tooltips to be displayed
- **tooltipLabel**: `ITooltipLabelType`
  - Customize the tooltip content
- **interactionKeys**: `string[]`
  - Sets the column names of data to interact with
- **showGridlines**: `boolean`
  - Toggles whether to display background map gridlines
- **suppressEvents**: `boolean`
  - Suppresses and disables all event emitters. Setting to true can increase performance for non-interactive charts.
- **clickEvent**: `any`
  - Set a callback for click event on each data point.
- **hoverEvent**: `any`
  - Set a callback to execute when mouse enters the chart.
- **mouseOutEvent**: `any`
  - Set a callback to execute when mouse leaves the chart.
- **initialLoadEvent**: `any`
  - Set a callback to execute when chart initially loads.
- **initialLoadEndEvent**: `any`
  - Set a callback to execute when chart's initial load ends.
- **drawStartEvent**: `any`
  - Set a callback to execute when chart drawing starts.
- **drawEndEvent**: `any`
  - Set a callback to execute when chart drawing ends.
- **transitionEndEvent**: `any`
  - Set a callback to execute when chart geometry transitions end.
- **hoverHighlight**: `object`
  - * @controlName TextArea
- **clickHighlight**: `object[]`
  - * @controlName TextArea
